claim: A PostCompact hook shall run `hotam gen-spec` after every auto-compact so the post-compact prompt reload reads fresh substrate-derived CLAUDE.md, not the stale pre-compact version.
why: Not yet ported in the Go port: the Go CLI (cmd/hotam) is a pure command-line tool with no host-hook / PostCompact integration, so there is no mechanism that automatically runs gen-spec after an auto-compact event. Auto-compact rewrites session context but does not re-read CLAUDE.md unless triggered. Without this hook, the operator post-compact runs on summary + stale CLAUDE.md. The historical Python mechanism was a PostCompact hook in .claude/settings.local.json running gen_spec.py; the Go equivalent would invoke `hotam gen-spec` from a host-side hook, but no such hook is wired in this port. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists in the Go port.
